patch_description: This redesign replaces the 1D-CNN with an adaptive bidirectional LSTM architecture that better captures temporal gait dynamics. It introduces enhanced feature engineering with clinical biomarkers (velocity, acceleration, range-of-motion, asymmetry) and handles variable sequence lengths naturally. The model uses predictive normalization for training stability and includes dropout for regularization. A masked global pooling converts LSTM outputs to fixed-length representations for classification. This approach improves on the original by better modeling temporal dependencies in gait patterns and incorporating domain-specific clinical features critical for Parkinson's assessment.
